hooks.prompt: Analyze this user request and determine which guardrail documents Claude MUST read before proceeding. Categories: 1) feature/change -> REQUIREMENTS.md (identify affected FR-*/NFR-*/ARCH-* IDs, present to user before implementing). 2) debug/error -> DEPENDENCIES.md (blast radius) + RUNBOOK.md (playbook). 3) aws/terraform/infra -> AWS_STATE.md (internet exposure map, safety rules, sensitive file list). 4) adapter/source changes -> SOURCES.yaml (TOS status, must be compliant). 5) security-relevant -> SECURITY.md (controls matrix). 6) schema/database -> infra/SCHEMA_TYPES.md + infra/schema.sql. 7) policy decision (IAM, models, SG) -> docs/adr/ (check existing, create new). Output format: 'BEFORE PROCEEDING: Read [file1], [file2]. This request affects [area]. Key requirements: [FR-X.Y.Z, NFR-X.Y.Z].' If the request is a simple question needing no guardrails, output: 'No guardrail docs needed.'

If this is a 'git commit' command, remind Claude to verify these before committing (informational, do not block): 1) Auth on all /api/* endpoints 2) sanitize_for_prompt() on LLM calls 3) enforce_pii_boundary() on RDS writes 4) README.md current 5) No unauthed endpoints. Respond ALLOW always — the automated pre-commit-audit.sh handles blocking.
